title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix adds 'Little House on the Prairie', a wholesome adaptation of Laura Ingalls Wilder's classic tale.
'The Long Walk', a Stephen King adaptation about a deadly competition, is now streaming on HBO Max.
'The Five-Star Weekend', a drama starring Jennifer Garner, is available on Peacock, filmed in Nantucket.

title: A 4th Red Sox player has been named a 2026 MLB All-Star
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ceddanne Rafaela of the Red Sox has been named to the 2026 MLB All-Star team, replacing injured Aaron Judge.
Rafaela, a center fielder, is hitting .283 with eight home runs and 39 RBIs through 87 games this season.
He joins Red Sox teammates Willson Contreras, Ranger Suarez, and Aroldis Chapman as All-Stars on the American League team.

title: Joey Chestnut to compete in inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Joey Chestnut to compete in inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship in Seabrook, New Hampshire, on July 11, 2026.
The competition will feature Kowloon's signature Saugus Wings, named after the city where the Kowloon restaurant is located.
Geoffrey Esper, current chicken wing record holder, will compete against Chestnut in the 10-minute wing eating contest.

title: France-Morocco was a fitting end to a spectacular World Cup in Boston
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: France defeated Morocco 2-0 in the final World Cup match held in Foxborough, with goals from Mbappé and Dembélé.
Boston hosted seven World Cup matches, showcasing teams like France, England, Morocco, and Germany over 27 days.
The event was seen as a success for the city, attracting international fans and enhancing Boston's global profile.

title: Gloucester woman accused of plotting to kill childhood friend using WD-40
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Phyllis Palazzola allegedly attempted to kill childhood friend Marianna DiMercurio using a WD-40-soaked rag in Gloucester.
Palazzola borrowed over $160,000 from DiMercurio to cover legal fees for a prior insurance fraud case.
Palazzola faces indictment on charges including attempted murder and witness intimidation; arraignment is set for July 21.

title: How a Waltham barber became France’s go-to during the World Cup
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Waltham barber Coco Fernandez became a trusted stylist for France's national soccer team during the 2026 World Cup.
Fernandez began cutting hair for stars like Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé following an unexpected WhatsApp message.
He continues serving the French team as they use Boston as a home base for the tournament.

title: Red Sox face lengthy travel issues ahead of series vs. Mets
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox faced a 17-hour delay in Chicago, departing at 3 p.m. ET for New York.
The team experienced multiple plane issues, delaying their flight to the Mets series.
The Red Sox-Mets game remains scheduled for 7:15 p.m. despite travel disruptions.

title: Photos: See breathtaking tall ships sail toward Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Over 60 international tall ships will sail into Boston Harbor for the six-day Sail Boston event starting July 11, 2026.
The ships, part of the Sail250 tour, will dock at various waterfront locations in Boston after arriving from New York.
The German tall ship Gorch Fock and others competed in the Five Sisters Race on their route to Boston.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Tight ends edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Hunter Henry remains the Patriots' primary tight end, coming off one of his most productive NFL seasons.
New faces include rookies Eli Raridon and Tanner Arkin, with Jack Westover transitioning back to tight end.
Depth behind Henry is limited; the group is young and will likely benefit from his experience and leadership.

title: Q&A: Gigi Perez reflects on Berklee, Boston, and a special Fenway week alongside Noah Kahan
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Gigi Perez's hit 'Sailor Song' went viral on TikTok, reaching the Spotify Billions Club and peaking at No. 22 on the Billboard Hot 100.
Perez, a former Berklee College of Music student, opened for Noah Kahan at his sold-out Fenway Park shows, performing and dueting on stage.
Perez discussed the impact of Berklee on her music career and her mental health practices while touring with Kahan's 'The Great Divide' tour.
